Action Items: - [ ] Z47/DVC to consult with Confido about their experience with Nixo - [ ] Z47/DVC to provide investment decision within 48-72 hours - [ ] Akash to introduce Sidhu/Chetan to Priya - [ ] Priya to connect with investors via WhatsApp A good evening. How should we do the intro folks? There are three of us today. Should I just introduce the fund and then everyone give one two lines about themselves? This one is also end of call commit or we have time? Don't have time but not necessarily end of call. Okay. Because given Anand is deeply plugged in so we can be patient. Okay. No, not yet. No, no, I'm just checking. Oh. Hello Priya. Hi. Hi, very nice to meet you guys. Nice to meet you. Is Preeti also joining? No, she unfortunately can't make it. She has some other important work to attend to, but I'll be here. Got it, got it. No, so quick context setting here. Akash Rajat Rao will quickly share a bit about us. We are Z47 and DVC, earlier we were known as Matrix Partners India. We invest out of two funds, one is a venture fund, which is more post-seed-seed to Series B. And DVC is a pre-seed-seed fund, where we partner with founders super early in their journey. Pertaining to what you are building, this is a space we're pretty deeply calibrated in. At one end for customer success and CESPM, we have a company called Rocket Lane, which we had seeded and then let the A and the B in, which was together with ABC. That was from the venture fund. And outside of that in the depl...

Next Steps from Rahul: - Introductions to consumer brand founders (Mohit Sadani and others) who successfully used mom influencer GTM channel - Market work and customer chats over next 48 hours - Follow-up discussion with Rajat to determine what's needed for next meeting - Overall positive reaction: found the concept "refreshingly unique" compared to typical consumer brand pitches He was my junior at IIT Gansur. So that's when I got in touch with him, met Raghav, Atulia and have been at Judo for the last 5 years. So you know about that part of me. Right. So now, sort of decided to move on. Yeah. Because Preksha has been at this Nomado for I think close to six, eight months now and we did some pilot and there was some conviction to sort of start this up. Yeah. But maybe, Priyaksha, you can talk about your background. So now a very similar profile on paper, very different skills. in practice but yes Engineering background, then worked at CS for a couple of years, then IMB, MBA in '17. Then I joined McKinsey. I was with McKinsey for about a couple of years And then I, after that I also wanted to at that point I joined OYO. They had launched Mountania for upscale hotels and they wanted somebody to create a full operations team for that and how would everything look like. I joined work with Vivek Sinha for a brief period in India and then moved to a role in UK because Rishabh had decided to shift to UK so I also decided to shift along with him. And so they created a role for me whe...

Go-to-Market Strategy: - Focus on national jurisdiction enterprises (80% of litigation) - Target PSUs and government bodies - Key differentiators: - Speed of filing - Digital experience - Cost advantage - 12-month team growth target: 20 lawyers (5 specialists, 15 process), 20 annotators, 5 tech team
Company Background & Team: - Founded in 2024 by 25-year-old founder from Bangalore - Strong legal connections through family: - Mother is expert in setting up law schools - Co-founder Pratik runs own law firm for 5 years - Third co-founder Takshu (currently in USA) - Initial pivot from education tech to legal tech in Jan 2024
Product & Technology: - AI-powered legal drafting system with human-in-loop verification - Key features: - Translation services ($1/page, 7000 pages/month from Karnataka High Court) - Real-time dashboard for enterprise clients - 14-day timeline to add new legal specializations - 12 annotator/proofreaders (non-lawyers) for quality control - Demo showed automated legal document drafting system

Lots of FF traits - had done shallow outside in work (i.e. public sources); didn’t dig into own YC network

OnCall Owl — summary (from the 2‑pager): - **What it is:** An **autonomous AI “on-call engineer”** that can **detect, diagnose, mitigate, verify, and document** production incidents without paging a human. - **Problem:** Modern distributed systems still rely on humans for on-call. The deck claims **60–80% of incidents are repetitive/deterministic**, while **SRE cost is high** (e.g., $180K+ senior SRE salary; **$5M–$15M annual SRE spend** at ~$500M ARR SaaS). - **How it works (capabilities):** - **Detects** anomalies from observability tools (Datadog, CloudWatch, Prometheus, Grafana) - **Diagnoses** via correlating deploy history, metrics, traces, config changes - **Mitigates** via actions like rollback, scale, restart, feature flag, traffic shift, failover, within guardrails - **Verifies** recovery before closing incident - **Learns** by drafting RCA, updating runbooks, creating follow-up tickets - Safety model: customers define **safety zones + approval thresholds**, with a **copilot → autonomy** progression. - **Why it matters / positioning:** Framed as **headcount replacement (labor leverage)** rather than incremental tooling. Target is to reduce L1/L2 on-call burden and MTTR, and be **infra-agnostic** across clouds/Kubernetes and the observability stack (not vendor-locked). - **Business model (deck numbers):** - “Free to start” (connect stack, observe first shift) - Pricing shown as **$500/shift/month** (also mentions **$99/mo/shift** earlier) plus **consumption/credit pa...

AI Notes: **OnFinance journey and challenges** **2025-04-28T14:32:00.000+05:30** Himanshu joined OnFinance after reconnecting with Anuj, a childhood acquaintance from the same locality with whom he shared a friend group but wasn't particularly close. Their relationship began with Himanshu providing GTM consultation before joining full-time to handle go-to-market while Anuj focused on fundraising and Priyesh on building the technology. **Company Evolution:** OnFinance began as a Singapore entity focused on crypto: - Initial product was a crypto and US equity advisory platform with a proprietary recommendation engine - Scaled to 100,000 users and accumulated $50M in AUM - Struggled to monetize with a subscription model - Raised angel funding for this initial product - Pivoted to Compliance OS after conversations with banks, Smallcase, Angel One, and other financial institutions **Leadership and Working Style:** The founding team operated with notable characteristics: - Anuj is described as persuasive with banks and skilled at leveraging his network - Strong BITS alumni connections helped secure initial meetings - Creative networking approaches including taking golf course membership to connect with potential clients - Culture of gift-giving to prospects (e.g., champagne for good meetings) - Work environment involved 24/7 schedules with team living together in a shared flat **Internal Challenges:** Several issues affected the company culture: - Poor conflict resolution processes...

Evaluation Plan + Key Questions to answer: 1. **What does the company do?** 1. 1. **Do we (DeVC/Z47) have a thesis on this space?** 1. 1. **What are the top 3 questions to diligence?** 1. 1. **Who has piped this company to us? What are their views?** 1. 1. **What do refs say about the founder?** 1. 1. **What's the DeVC team views?** 1. 1. **What are the Collective signals?** 1. 1. **Why is this company likely to be special?** 1. 1. **Who can help? Experts / Venture Partners?** 1. 1. **Deal dynamics? Potential deal to do?** 1. ---

Analysis by dev - 2026-01-08: ### Company Snapshot Problem & solution OpusCollect positions itself as an AI-first full-stack collections platform aimed at moving from pre-delinquency to hard-bucket collections, addressing a market with fragmented pan-India field operations and little scale in field-based collections. Market notes emphasize a gap in pan-India field collectors and a shift toward AI-enabled, data-driven recovery processes for banks and NBFCs. Ideal customer profile and pricing Direct clients include Unity Bank, Jana Bank, Avanti Finance, BikeBazaar, Branch, with additional through-partner channels (Fusion, ESAF, Cashe). Total assets under management ~₹900 crore; monthly collections ~₹6 crore. Pricing signals: - Take rates reported as 10% regular, 15% SMA, 20% for 90+ DP buckets. - Revenue drivers indicate 60–70% commission from collections, 15–18% automated legal services, 5–8% AI-driven collections, 2–8% data enrichment; for >90DPD, commissions can be 25–35% of amount collected. - For Kissht, a go-live target of ₹1 crore per month billing is noted. [Inference] Pricing appears to mix performance-based commissions with verticalized service fees; no formal external pricing sheet disclosed. Status of product & traction highlight Live, multi-channel field operations with AI/automation components: - In-house bot calling, route optimization, automated case allocation, and digital legal workflow. - Android field-collection app; real-time field tracking; 64k digital leg...

Heads up note from @Rahul Mathur 2025-08-23T15:18:00.000+05:30: Hi team, Sharing the heads-up note for our investment in [Orange Slice AI](https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/orange-slice). The company provides AI agents that find you customers who show demand signals - the agents crawl the internet for signals/firmographic data that help sales teams find high-intent prospects by continuously monitoring the internet for buying signals and firmographic changes DeVC is investing $250K as part of a post-YC SAFE round at a $25M cap with MFN clause **Founders** 1. [Kishan Sripada](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kishansripada/) (Co-Founder & CTO) 1. 1 YOE as SDE Intern @ Ramp 1. Ran a successful dance formation app used by Disney, Broadway, Lakers etc 1. Ran an “Airbnb for kitchen space” business during COVID 1. Market making platform connecting unused restaurant kitchens with home cooks 1. Sold to restaurant chain Bravo for $150K 1. Ref from Ramp Manager: "Good kid but young and hard market” 1. In context: "This guy (the manager) is harsh so even a terse vote of approval like this is strong!” 1. UMich ‘25 - dropout 1. [Vihaar Nandigala](https://www.linkedin.com/in/vihaarnandigala/) (Co-Founder & CEO) 1. Left $500K TC placement at JPMC to start this business 1. UMich ‘25 - dropout **Investing Process** 1. This was sourced by simultaneously DeVC IP team (YC mining) & Z47 Enterprise AI team 1. Ashwin & Krish did the 1st call from a Product Eval POV - post which he shared with the wide...
